## Session-start sync check

Near the start of every session, run once:
```bash
cd ../ai-scaffolding && git fetch --quiet && git rev-parse --short HEAD
```
If the result matches `49d5e25`, you're current; carry on.

If the result differs, this CLAUDE.md is stale. Refresh it by running:
```bash
../ai-scaffolding/scripts/sync.sh $(basename "$PWD")
```

**Then start a new Claude Code session before doing real work.** Re-reading CLAUDE.md mid-session leaves the old version in context alongside the new one; only a fresh session loads the updated file cleanly. The new session will pick up the refreshed CLAUDE.md automatically.

Run this check once per session, not on every prompt.

# Coding Style & Conventions

These are always-apply rules. For fuller guidance (idioms, review checklist, per-language patterns) use the **`/coding-standards`** skill.

## Git Conventions

- **Commit messages:** Imperative mood, descriptive summary line, details in body
- **Branches:** `feature/<short-slug>`
- **Don't push without asking** — builds may be running
- **Don't force-push** to shared branches

## Code Patterns

- **Feature gating:** Use claims/feature flags — never gate by email domain
- **Versioning:** CalVer where applicable (e.g. `26.0.0` = year 2026, first release)
- **TypeScript:** Prefer strict typing. Use interfaces for API response shapes.
- **State management:** Redux Toolkit (RTK) for React apps
- **NZ/AU English** in user-facing strings (organisation, licence, centralised)

## Security

- Never expose internal API endpoints, database schemas, or architecture details in user-facing code or docs
- Never commit secrets, API keys, or credentials
- Use environment variables for all configuration that varies between environments

## Testing

- Write tests for new features
- Update snapshots when UI changes are intentional (`yarn test -u` or equivalent)
- Run the full test suite before pushing

## CLI Tools

- **Always scope CLI calls to the target project/repo per-command** — never switch the active/default project in global CLI config. Multiple agents may be running concurrently, and switching the global project in one session affects all others.
  - `gh` — use `--repo owner/repo` (not `gh repo set-default`)
  - `gcloud` — use `--project=PROJECT_ID` (not `gcloud config set project`)
  - `firebase` — use `--project=PROJECT_ID`
  - `vercel` — use `--scope` or project flags
  - `fly` — use `--app=APP_NAME`
  - `kubectl` — use `--context` and `--namespace` flags
  - `aws` — use `--profile` and `--region` flags
  - `terraform` — use `-chdir` or workspace flags
  - Any other CLI — prefer the per-command project/scope flag over setting a global default

## General

- Keep PRs focused on a single concern
- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones
- Don't add unnecessary dependencies
- Follow existing patterns in each repo — consistency within a project trumps personal preference
